title: Starship underpins SpaceX’s growth ambitions
summary: SpaceX's upcoming Starship test flight is crucial for the company's growth ambitions, as highlighted in its recent prospectus. The vehicle is seen as a key driver for SpaceX's future plans.

title: The internet was ‘too expensive’ too
summary: The article discusses how early infrastructure platforms often appear uneconomic due to high costs, immature supply chains, and lack of scaling. It draws a parallel with the early internet, which was initially considered too expensive.

title: CEO Series: Open Cosmos’ Rafel Jorda Siquier on finding a niche in space.
summary: Open Cosmos' CEO Rafel Jorda Siquier discusses combining Earth observation, connectivity, and IoT into a single platform in an interview with SpaceNews. The conversation highlights the company's strategy to find a niche in the space industry.

title: LatConnect 60 announces accelerated growth investment round to build AUKUS-aligned Highest Resolution SWIR Satellite constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 (LC60) is raising funds to accelerate the development of a high-resolution SWIR satellite constellation aligned with AUKUS. The company, based in Perth, Australia, focuses on Earth observation and AI.

title: Space Force eyes 2027 demonstrations of in-space refueling and satellite servicing
summary: The U.S. Space Force plans to conduct in-space refueling and satellite servicing demonstrations by 2027, with the USSF-23 mission launching vehicles to test these capabilities in geostationary orbit.

title: SpaceX files for IPO
summary: SpaceX has filed documents for an initial public offering (IPO) on May 20, revealing its financial details and ambitious plans. This marks a significant step in the company's transition to becoming a publicly traded entity.

title: U.S. ‘more prepared’ for next WRC
summary: U.S. officials state they are better prepared for the next World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC) to defend spectrum priorities. The conference will address key radio-frequency spectrum issues.

title: Orbital Data Centers: Power and Thermal Management for Scalable Architectures
summary: A new whitepaper by Redwire explores scalable power generation and thermal management as key technologies for orbital data centers. The document highlights challenges and solutions for power and heat management in space-based computing infrastructures.

title: Starfighters turns Texas facility toward microgravity flight testing
summary: Starfighters Space is converting its Texas facility to support microgravity flight testing in response to NASA's request for commercial parabolic flight capabilities. The company is leveraging its F-104 supersonic jets, originally designed for satellite air-launch, for this new purpose.

title: Report finds U.S. space supply chains rely heavily on Chinese manufacturing
summary: A report by Altana reveals that U.S. space supply chains are heavily dependent on Chinese manufacturing, with additional reliance on Taiwanese semiconductors and upstream exposure to China and Russia.

title: Strange stacked stones spotted on Mars | Space photo of the day for May 21, 2026
summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover discovered a stack of rocks on the Martian surface. The unusual geological formation has caught the attention of scientists and space enthusiasts.

title: NASA exoplanet-hunting spacecraft TESS reveals its most complete look at the night sky yet
summary: NASA's TESS spacecraft has released its most comprehensive view of the night sky, identifying 6,000 potential exoplanets beyond our solar system. This marks a significant milestone in exoplanet research and discovery.

title: SpaceX will launch its 1st-ever Starship V3 megarocket today. The stakes couldn't be higher
summary: SpaceX is set to launch its first-ever Starship V3 megarocket today, with significant implications for NASA's lunar ambitions. The mission carries high stakes as it marks a critical step for SpaceX's next-generation rocket.

title: Space debris is forcing satellites to dodge more often — costing us vital science. 'Things will get worse before they get better'
summary: Space debris is increasingly forcing satellites to perform avoidance maneuvers, resulting in lost data and fuel. Experts warn that the situation will worsen before improving.

title: Scientists find a hidden route to the moon that saves fuel
summary: An international team of researchers discovered a more fuel-efficient route to the moon using advanced computer modeling. This breakthrough could significantly reduce the cost and complexity of lunar missions.

title: SpaceX stacks Starship V3 rocket, completes major fueling test ahead of debut launch
summary: SpaceX has stacked its Starship spacecraft and Super Heavy booster at Starbase ahead of the rocket's 12th test flight. The company also completed a major fueling test for the Starship V3 rocket.

title: US military test-launches nuclear-capable ICBM from California (photos)
summary: The U.S. military conducted a test launch of an unarmed intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on May 20, 2026. This test was part of routine evaluations to ensure the reliability and readiness of the nuclear-capable system.

title: Where did Neptune's mysterious moon Nereid come from? It may be the only survivor of the planet's violent history
summary: Neptune's moon Nereid may be the sole survivor of the planet's original satellite system, according to a new study. Researchers suggest it could provide insights into Neptune's violent past.

title: Venus and the crescent moon dazzle in a spectacular post-sunset show (photos)
summary: The moon and Venus aligned in the western sky on May 18-19, creating a visually stunning celestial event captured in photos.

title: NASA Welcomes 16th Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson
summary: Matt Anderson was sworn in as NASA's 16th deputy administrator during a ceremony in Washington. He will assist in implementing the President's national space policy and advancing U.S. leadership in space.

title: NASA Highlights 2025 International Space Station Science Results
summary: NASA released the 2025 Annual Highlights of Results in May 2026, showcasing over 750 ISS investigations from 2025 that advanced space life science and Earth benefits. The report highlights research achievements from the past year.

title: Lunar Robots: NASA Spotlights Moon Base at 2026 FIRST Robotics Competition
summary: NASA highlighted the importance of robotics in its Moon base plans at the 2026 FIRST Robotics Competition, emphasizing opportunities for future engineers. The event showcased how robotics will support NASA's long-term lunar presence goals.

title: New Eruption in the Bismarck Sea
summary: Satellite imagery has detected a new volcanic eruption in the Bismarck Sea near Papua New Guinea. This event highlights the importance of space-based monitoring for natural disaster detection.

title: NASA Announces Winners in University Aeronautics Competition
summary: NASA announced the winners of its fifth annual Gateways to Blue Skies Competition, with a South Dakota State University team taking first place for their innovative aircraft maintenance solution. The competition challenged students to develop systems that could advance commercial aircraft maintenance.

title: La NASA informará sobre su estrategia y misiones para la Base Lunar
summary: NASA will hold a press conference on May 26 to discuss its strategy and missions for the Lunar Base, highlighting progress toward a sustainable lunar surface presence.

title: NASA TechLeap Prize: Robotically Manipulated Payload Challenge
summary: NASA's Robotically Manipulated Payload Challenge, part of the TechLeap Prize series, seeks payloads for robotic arm manipulation in low Earth orbit to advance in-space servicing and manufacturing. Up to three winners will receive funding to develop their proposals.

title: Psyche Spacecraft Completes Mars Flyby
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft successfully completed a close flyby of Mars on May 15, capturing images of the planet's surface. The flyby included observations of the double-ring crater Huygens and the heavily cratered southern highlands.

title: NASA-developed AI Could Help Track Harmful Algae
summary: NASA scientists developed an AI tool that fuses satellite data to detect harmful algal blooms in Florida and California. The technology addresses a longstanding challenge in ocean monitoring.

title: NASA to Provide Update on Moon Base Strategy, Missions
summary: NASA will hold a news conference to update the public on its Moon Base strategy and plans for a sustained lunar presence. The briefing will include discussions on progress, new industry partners, and mission details.

title: Starship Flight 12: Welcome to the Show Block 3 and Pad 2
summary: Starship Flight 12 marks a significant milestone with the introduction of Block 3 and Pad 2, seven years after the first Raptor engine test. This event highlights advancements in SpaceX's Starship program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's Starship Flight 12, Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Vega C missions.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship. It highlights the ongoing changes and advancements in this key component of future space missions.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is progressing with planning and processing for Artemis III, a significant crewed mission. The mission includes key tests for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for the debut of Flight 12, marking the next evolution of its Starship second stage, which has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company surpassed all its 2025 milestones.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the ISS on its third attempt, following the docking of Progress MS-34. The launch marks another resupply mission to the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China have busy launch schedules with nine launches planned across the U.S. and China during the week of May 11. The missions span various activities in the space industry.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and develop materials for spacecraft to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to support ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, and public engagement. This technology is opening new pathways for space exploration and education.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical parachute test for a space mission, involving a high-temperature baking process to ensure reliability. This test is part of preparations for an upcoming launch.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars, sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 launch.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for space applications, enabling better handling of objects in orbit. This innovation could enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully passed space-like tests, remaining on schedule for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has approved two Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research with cost-effective solutions. These missions demonstrate ESA's commitment to affordable yet innovative space science.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67 billion in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO. The company is expected to go public next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system could accelerate the nuclear arms race, despite its intended purpose of protecting America.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve collaborations with Chinese researchers, posing potential security risks. The findings highlight concerns over sensitive technology sharing.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in design reviews. The company aims to enhance efficiency in engineering production by addressing inefficiencies in how design decisions are made.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: A report by CSF forecasts a potential shortage in launch supply, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000 depending on the scenario, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, announced the launch of a new satellite bus business line. The initiative targets low-cost, high-volume, and high-power satellite missions across communications, Earth observation, and national security programs.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Solestial
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring Solestial to expand its capabilities. This marks another significant merger and acquisition activity for York Space Systems in 2024.

title: Astrolab Unveils Payloads Flying on FLIP Lunar Mission
summary: Astrolab has announced the payloads that will fly on its upcoming FLIP lunar mission. The company plans to send a rover to the Moon later this year.

title: Potomac Database Systems Unveils Plans to Amass Lunar Data
summary: Potomac Database Systems has announced plans to collect and store lunar data, highlighting commercial opportunities beyond traditional lunar activities like mining and infrastructure development.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport management. This collaboration aims to enhance airport operations using Spire's aviation intelligence.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX has filed initial documents for an IPO, providing the public with its first detailed financial disclosures. This move offers unprecedented insight into the company's financial health ahead of going public.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: China's hosting of the 2027 World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC-27) raises concerns among U.S. space operators due to its potential influence over critical satellite agenda items. The event is expected to heavily impact the growing space sector.

title: Redwire Taps Voyager for Subcontract for DARPA Otter Mission
summary: Redwire has awarded Voyager Technologies a subcontract for DARPA's Otter mission to supply its high-precision Acceleration Measurement System (AMS). The mission aims to demonstrate advanced technologies in space.

title: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon CEOs Want Multiple Constellations for D2D Service
summary: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon CEOs discussed their satellite strategies and emphasized the need for multiple constellations to support direct-to-device (D2D) services after announcing a joint venture for collaboration.

title: Iceye Launches New Solution for Banking Sector
summary: Iceye has launched a new natural catastrophe monitoring solution tailored for the international banking sector. This offering aims to provide risk assessment and mitigation services for financial institutions.

title: HackerOne Exec Explains Key Security Trends in the Age of AI
summary: A HackerOne executive discusses how generative AI is being weaponized by hackers in cybersecurity trends over the past year. The article highlights the growing threat AI poses to security in the space industry.

title: Securing the Return to the Moon
summary: The article discusses the importance of learning from past events to secure a sustainable return to the Moon. It highlights the need for strategic planning and international cooperation in lunar missions.

title: Sandhoo Named SDA Director, PAE for Missile Warning and Tracking
summary: Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo was officially named director of the Space Development Agency (SDA) effective May 11, following his acting role since September. The SDA also announced a procurement action (PAE) for missile warning and tracking capabilities.

title: Northrop Grumman’s First MRV Readies for Summer Launch to Expand the Space Servicing Toolkit
summary: Northrop Grumman's SpaceLogistics is preparing to launch its first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) this summer for on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension. The MRV aims to expand the company's space servicing capabilities.
